channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Workers Comp Fraud: Altered Medical and Wage PDFs
item.description: Originally published at htpbe.tech. The version on htpbe.tech stays in sync with the latest detection algorithm — refer to it for the canonical text. The National Insurance Crime Bureau puts US...

item.title: Migrating a Vite i18n App to Next.js Without Breaking Everything
item.description: The Architecture Shift: SPA vs. Framework Internationalization (i18n) is one of those features that feels straightforward in a Single Page Application (SPA). You install react-i18next, wrap your...

item.title: Diagnosing Cloudflare Blocks Before Changing Your Scraper
item.description: A scraper fails, someone swaps the User-Agent, someone else adds a proxy, then the job starts passing locally but fails again in CI. That usually happens because Cloudflare did not block...

item.title: The Chatbot That Foretold Why People Share Secrets With ChatGPT
item.description: In the 1960s an MIT professor named Joseph Weizenbaum created a chatbot called ELIZA. The conversations people had with it set precedents for the chatbots to come.

item.title: Stop Arguing About Code Style — Set Up Prettier, ESLint & Husky Once
item.description: Why this matters I’ve worked on a few frontend projects where code reviews turned into style debates—tabs vs spaces, semicolons, quote styles… you name it. It slows everything down and adds zero...

item.title: The (no longer) missing multi-agent pattern: triggering dynamic workflows from an agent
item.description: When building multi-agent systems, rigid state graphs quickly fall apart in the face of dynamic user inputs. Imagine building a smart assistant: a user hands you a checklist of three household...

item.title: Why AI Agents Are Replacing Traditional SaaS
item.description: A few weeks ago I was setting up a new project and needed to do the usual dance: create a Notion doc, spin up a Linear board, invite the team to Slack, and set up a couple of Zapier automations to...

item.title: 🚀 How I Optimize Slow MySQL Queries in Laravel: My Practical Checklist
item.description: One of the most common questions I hear is: "My API is slow. Where do I start?" The first instinct is usually: Upgrade the server Increase CPU Add more RAM But in many cases, the database query is...

item.title: Getting the public IP in PHP — no dependencies, no API key
item.description: Getting the public IP in PHP — no dependencies, no API key PHP is still one of the most widely deployed server-side languages, running a significant share of the web's backend code. If you're...

item.title: Making ServiceLoader usable: a provider factory
item.description: I keep coming back to java.util.ServiceLoader. I have used it to put a JSON layer behind a contract, so the core code carries no direct dependency on any particular JSON library and I can swap the...

item.title: Learn 7 common MongoDB query mistakes with simple find() examples, including $or, $in, nested fields, dates, and text search.
item.description: 7 MongoDB Query Mistakes That Return the Wrong Results VisuaLeaf VisuaLeaf VisuaLeaf Follow Jul 14 7 MongoDB Query Mistakes That Return the Wrong Results #mongodb #coding #software #database 3...

item.title: Grok Build Uploads Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Reads
item.description: xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed. A researcher...

item.title: DOGE Used AI for Housing Policy. The Government Won’t Say How
item.description: In response to a public records request, HUD has withheld documents about DOGE’s use of AI—in part by citing a privilege that doesn’t exist.

item.title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
item.description: New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state...

item.title: U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious...

item.title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
item.description: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six...

item.title: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet
item.description: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from...

item.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
item.description: Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform....

item.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
item.description: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

item.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
item.description: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation,...

item.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
item.description: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

item.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
item.description: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

item.title: Microsoft tests Windows Search without all the ads and fluff
item.description: Microsoft is testing a cleaner version of the Windows 11 search menu that strips it of recommended content and ads. In a blog post on Monday, Microsoft announced that it's rolling out the...

item.title: OnePlus is reportedly bailing on the US
item.description: OnePlus and its parent company, Oppo, plan to announce in the coming days that OnePlus brand will be leaving the US and European markets, according to a machine translation of a WinFuture report....

item.title: Siri AI Is Becoming Apple’s Everything Tool
item.description: Apple’s revamped Siri is more than a voice assistant; it’s now the backbone of the iPhone user experience. You can try it now through the iOS 27 public beta.

item.title: A two-pack of DJI’s most capable wireless mics just got its first price cut
item.description: Smartphones these days have incredible cameras that are capable of taking smooth, sharp video, but the microphones are often lacking, to say the least. A wireless lavalier microphone can...

item.title: The US government warns that Russia state hackers are coming after your router
item.description: With residential proxies all the rage, CISA urges router users to be vigilant.

item.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
item.description: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell...

item.title: The Shokz OpenRun Pro are the cheapest they’ve been since January
item.description: Noise-canceling earbuds are great for flights and focusing, but they're not always ideal for outdoor workouts. The last-gen Shokz OpenRun Pro's open-ear design lets you enjoy music while staying...

item.title: The Pixel colors might rule this year
item.description: This year's Google Pixel 11 lineup might come in a bunch of funky colors. A series of now-deleted Amazon listings spotted by 9to5Google show what appear to be placeholders for Google's upcoming...
